data.title: Innventure, Inc. closes $40M offering of common stock to repay outstanding convertible debentures.
data.description: Innventure anticipates using the funds raised from the offering to completely repay all remaining convertible debentures.

data.title: NEJM editorial praises Science Corporation's PRIMA implant as first to restore vision in AMD patients. AMD affects 5M+ globally.
data.description: An editorial released in The New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM) today highlighted Science Corporation’s PRIMA implant as the initial therapy in clinical trials to effectively enhance functional vision in specific patients with geographic atrophy (GA) stemming from age-related macular degeneration (AMD). AMD, a prominent cause of blindness, impacts over 5 million individuals globally. The editorial, titled Behind the Science: Restoring Vision for Patients with AMD, underscores the groundbreaking potential of this treatment.

data.title: Granite REIT buys C$256.1M properties, total acquisitions C$292M, dispositions C$190M, aiming for 6% yield.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Granite Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX: GRT.UN) disclosed the purchase of five income-generating properties in the United States, totaling around 1.2 million square feet. The combined acquisition cost was approximately $256.1 million (US$185.7 million). These properties were obtained at an initial weighted average yield of about 4.7%, with expectations to stabilize at a yield of roughly 6.0% within a two-year period.

data.title: Liberty Energy Inc. (NYSE: LBRT) to release Q4 and full year 2025 financial results on January 28, 2026, followed by a conference call on January 29, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. MT. CEO Ron Gusek to present.
data.description: In Denver, Liberty Energy Inc. (NYSE: LBRT) revealed plans to unveil its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year concluding on December 31, 2025. The announcement is scheduled for after market hours on Wednesday, January 28, 2026. A subsequent conference call to analyze the outcomes will take place on Thursday, January 29, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. Mountain Time (10:00 a.m. Eastern Time). Ron Gusek, the President and Chief Executive Officer, will be presenting the Company’s results during the call.

data.title: Biomerica reports Q2 fiscal 2026 results: Operating expenses down 4%, working capital up by 14.7%. Net sales at $1.21M, lower than $1.64M last year, due to distributor stocking. Medicare payment rate set at $300 for inFoods IBS test. PythonAI-backed inFoods IBS Trigger Food Navigator launched. Egyptian Drug Authority authorizes Biomerica's screening test portfolio. Cost management leads to $457K improvement in working capital. Strategic pivot towards higher-value products for significant margin expansion. Margin expected to grow from new partnerships and hp+detect™ opportunities. $2.5M in cash position. 27% reduction in R&D expenses while investing in commercial launch. Revenue softness attributed to transition, not fundamental deterioration. Access to additional capital for future growth initiatives. CEO and CFO comment on financial improvement.
data.description: Biomerica, Inc. reported its financial results for the second quarter of fiscal 2026, ending November 30, 2025. Operating expenses declined by 4% year-to-date, and the balance sheet strengthened with a 14.7% improvement in working capital. Key highlights included a collaboration with Henry Schein to market the inFoods™ IBS test, and the launch of an AI-backed inFoods™ IBS Trigger Food Navigator to support personalized IBS therapy. Additionally, post-quarter end, CMS established a national Medicare payment rate of $300 for the inFoods™ IBS test, and the Egyptian Drug Authority authorized Biomerica's screening test portfolio. Net sales for the second quarter were $1.21 million, down from $1.64 million in the previous year, attributed to distributor stocking changes. Management highlighted a strategic pivot towards higher-margin products and cost management efforts which improved the cash position to $2.5 million. Research and development expenses were reduced by 27%. These initiatives are expected to drive significant margin expansion in the future.

data.title: Market split with key losers Tesla, Broadcom, AppLovin, Wells Fargo; small caps hit new high. Nvidia chipmaker looming.
data.description: Stocks in the small-cap sector reached a record high, while major indexes faced downward pressure from Tesla, Broadcom, AppLovin, and Wells Fargo. Technology company Nvidia and chipmaker Taiwan Semi exerted influence on the market. Dow Jones Futures were divided as Tesla, Broadcom, AppLovin, and Wells Fargo experienced losses, with the spotlight on Nvidia's chipmaker sector.

data.title: Dow closes lower, Nasdaq big loser; small caps gain, robotics stock slides on preliminary results.
data.description: In the mid-afternoon on Wednesday, small cap stocks saw an increase in activity, while a robotics company experienced a decline in their stock value due to preliminary results. The stock market exhibited mixed performance for the day, with the Dow Jones closing lower and the Nasdaq experiencing significant losses. The specific robotics company in question faced a slide in its stock price, as reported in live coverage of the market events.

data.title: My Instant AI expands to 150+ countries, empowering 1000s of small retailers worldwide to sell AI services.
data.description: A technology platform headquartered in Scottsdale has announced the commencement of global retail distribution in over 150 countries. This move allows thousands of independent retailers to generate retail margin by selling AI access that operates on a cash basis. The platform, operating under the name My Instant AI, initiated the global retail distribution on January 14, 2026. This development opens up new avenues for retailers worldwide to tap into the benefits of AI technology, marking a significant milestone for the company's expansion efforts.

data.title: HOMB reports a strong quarter with $400M loan growth, sub-40% efficiency ratio, 18% income increase, and 2.10% annual ROA.
data.description: Home BancShares, Inc., parent company of Centennial Bank, released its quarterly earnings. In Q4 2025, net income was $118.2 million, with total revenue at $282.1 million. Net income steadily increased through the year, reaching $475.4 million annually, with diluted earnings per share at $0.60. PPNR also grew from $146.2 million in Q4 2024 to $167.7 million in Q4 2025. Non-performing assets to total assets were 0.55%. Total loans receivable were $15.69 billion, and total assets were $22.88 billion at the end of Q4 2025. The Company also repurchased shares and paid dividends. The acquisition of Mountain Commerce Bancshares, Inc. is expected to close in H1 2026. The Company's efficiency ratio was 39.54%, and it held various non-GAAP financial measures. Further details are available in the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nabors Industries Ltd. invites to Q4 2025 earnings conference call on Feb. 12 at 10:00 a.m. Central Time. CEO and CFO will lead.
data.description: Bermuda-based company Nabors Industries Ltd. (NYSE: NBR) has announced that Anthony G. Petrello, the Chairman, President, and CEO, along with Miguel Rodriguez, the CFO, will host an event on February 12, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Central Time. This event will be open to participation from the public.

data.title: Transneural Therapeutics presents preclinical data on TN-001 at ACNP 64th Annual Meeting (2026), showcasing promising results for MDD and PTSD. TN-001 induces neuroplasticity.
data.description: A recent poster presentation showcased encouraging pharmacological and behavioral findings of TN-001, a leading compound, in preliminary tests. TN-001 is being developed to address major depressive disorder (MDD) and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). The compound, which triggers neuroplasticity quickly, has demonstrated promising potential in preclinical studies.

data.title: Daktronics (Nasdaq: DAKT) to present at Sidoti's Micro-Cap Virtual Investor Conference on Jan 22, 2026.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, in Brookings, South Dakota, Daktronics Inc., a top designer and maker of dynamic video communication displays and control systems, stated that they will participate in Sidoti’s Micro-Cap Virtual Investor Conference on January 22, 2026. At the conference, the company's management will give a presentation and hold individual meetings with investors. Daktronics Inc. (Nasdaq: DAKT) is recognized for its high-quality products serving a global customer base.
